item.title: 深入了解 Spring Security 架构 | Dive into the Spring Security Architecture
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我们将研究构成 Spring Security 的组件并了解 Spring Security 架构的工作原理。 通过了解 Spring Security 的组件及其工作原理，配置和实现我们自己的安全机制就变得很容易。

以下是构成 Spring Security 架构的基本组件。

  • 过滤器
  • 认证管理器
  • 认证提供者
  • 用户详情服务
  • 密码编码器


让我们详细讨论一下它们中的每一个

过滤器

在 Spring 应用程序中，每个请求在到达控制器类之前都需要经过一系列过滤器。 这些过滤器负责对用户及其访问资源的请求进行身份验证和授权。
过滤器根据定义的内部规则检查每个请求的有效性。 您可以使用自己的规则创建自定义过滤器。

假设请求位于身份验证过滤器中。 身份验证过滤器负责提取用户身份验证详细信息和令牌。 这些用户详细信息被打包为身份验证对象并传递到身份验证管理器。
认证管理器


 public interface AuthenticationManager { Authentication authenticate(Authentication authentication) throws AuthenticationException; }

认证管理器从认证过滤器接收认证对象。 身份验证管理器是一个对象，其职责是找到对用户进行身份验证的方法。 这是通过使用Authentication Provider来实现的。

在典型的 Spring Security 配置中，只有一个Authentication Manager将身份验证请求委托给正确的Authentication Provider 。

身份验证管理器接口只有一种名为“authenticate”的方法，该方法接收从身份验证过滤器传递下来的身份验证对象。 成功验证用户身份后，验证管理器将返回经过验证的Authentication对象。

认证提供者


 public interface AuthenticationProvider { Authentication authenticate(Authentication authentication) throws AuthenticationException; boolean supports(Class<?> authentication); }

每个身份验证提供者负责处理不同的身份验证机制。 例如，Spring 应用程序可以同时具有用户名和密码身份验证以及 HttpBasic 身份验证。 对于这两种身份验证机制，都会有一个身份验证提供程序实现。

身份验证提供程序接口具有类似于身份验证管理器的身份验证方法和支持方法。
验证方法实际上验证用户的凭据或令牌。 此方法使用 UserDetailsS​​ervice 接口获取用户详细信息，该接口负责从类似用户存储的数据库中检索用户详细信息。 身份验证成功后，该方法将经过身份验证的Authentication对象返回给Authentication Manager 。 最后，这个经过身份验证的对象由身份验证管理器存储在 spring security 上下文中，该上下文保存用户的身份验证信息。 可以在整个应用程序中访问此信息。

用户详情服务

密码编码器

由于我们讨论的是安全性，因此在整个身份验证过程中保护用户密码的安全非常重要。 这使得我们开发人员可以更轻松地管理用户，同时对用户进行身份验证并将其存储在数据库中。

要使用密码编码器，请定义一个返回类型PasswordEncoder的bean

 @Bean public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() { return new BCryptPasswordEncoder(); }

现在，您可以将此 Bean 注入到用户服务中，对密码进行编码，然后将其存储在数据库中，并在身份验证提供程序中对密码进行解密以进行验证。

了解和理解 Spring Security 的这些组件有助于更好地为我们的应用程序实现安全性。

如果有错误请在评论中指出。

关注我： Twitter 、 LinkedIn 、 GitHub 、 Linktree



原文:

In this post, we'll be looking at the components that form spring security and understand how the spring security architecture works. By getting to know the components of spring security and their working, it becomes easy to configure and implement our own security mechanisms.

Components of Spring Security

The following are the basic components that form the spring security architecture.

  • Filters
  • Authentication Manager
  • Authentication Provider
  • User Details Service
  • Password Encoder


Let's talk about each one of them in detail

Filters

In spring applications, every request needs to pass through a chain of filters before the request ends up in the controller class. These filters are responsible for the authentication and authorization of users and their requests to access resources.
The filters check the validity of each request based on internal rules defined. You can create your custom filters with your own rules.

Let's say the request is in the authentication filter. The authentication filter is responsible for extracting user authentication details and tokens. These user details are packaged as authentication objects and passed to Authentication Manager.
The auth object is created using an implementation Authentication interface such as UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken.

Authentication Manager 


public interface AuthenticationManager { Authentication authenticate(Authentication authentication) throws AuthenticationException;
}

The Authentication Manager receives an authentication object from the authentication filter. Authentication Manager is an object whose responsibility is to find a way to authenticate users. That is achieved by using Authentication Provider.

In a typical spring security configuration, there is only one Authentication Manager which delegates the authentication request to the correct Authentication Provider.

The Authentication Manager interface has only one method called authenticate that takes in an authentication object passed down from the authentication filter. Upon successful authentication of the user, the Authentication Manager returns an authenticated Authentication object.

Authentication Provider 


public interface AuthenticationProvider { Authentication authenticate(Authentication authentication) throws AuthenticationException; boolean supports(Class<?> authentication);
}

There can be multiple Authentication Providers in a spring security context. Every Authentication Provider is responsible for handling different authentication mechanisms. For example, a spring application can have both username and password authentication as well as HttpBasic authentication. For both of these authentication mechanisms, there will be an Authentication Provider implementation.

The Authentication Provider interface has a authenticate method like the Authentication Manager and a supports method.
The supports method checks if the current provider supports the authentication for the given type of credentials, if not the credentials are passed to the next provider/filter.

The authenticate method actually validates the credentials or tokens of the user. This method gets the user details using the UserDetailsService interface which is responsible for retrieving user details from a user store-like database. Upon successful authentication, the method returns an authenticated Authentication object back to the Authentication Manager. At last, this authenticated object is stored by the Authentication Manager in the spring security context, which holds the users' authentication information. This information can be accessed throughout the application.

UserDetailsService

The UserDetailsService interface has only one method loadUserByUsername which takes the username from the authentication object passed down from the filter.

The loadUserByUsername method returns a UserDetials object which contains user details like username, password, authorities and, other details.

public interface UserDetailsService { UserDetails loadUserByUsername(String username) throws UsernameNotFoundException;
}

public interface UserDetails extends Serializable { Collection<? extends GrantedAuthority> getAuthorities(); String getPassword(); String getUsername(); boolean isAccountNonExpired(); boolean isAccountNonLocked(); boolean isCredentialsNonExpired(); boolean isEnabled();
}

Password Encoder

Since we are talking about security, it is very important that the users' passwords are secured for the entirety of the authentication process. Spring Security comes with some de facto password encoders out of the box. This makes it easier for us developers to manage users while authenticating and storing them in the database.

Some popular implementations of the PasswordEncoder interface are BCryptPasswordEncoder, SCryptPasswordEncoder, and AbstractPasswordEncoder.

To work with the password encoder, define a bean that returns a type PasswordEncoder

@Bean public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() { return new BCryptPasswordEncoder(); }

Now you can inject this bean in the user service to encode the password before storing it in the database and in the authentication provider to decrypt the password for validation.

Knowing and understanding these components of spring security helps to implement security better for our applications.

If made any mistakes please point it out in the comments.

Follow me on : Twitter, LinkedIn, GitHub, Linktree

item.title: Go 入门 (golang) | Getting Started With Go (golang)
item.description:
与所有编程语言一样，有大量选项可用于编写您希望完成的软件或自动化，这对于新程序员或刚接触某种语言的人来说可能会很麻烦。 幸运的是，总有一个地方可以让您快速掌握一门新语言。 例如，所有语言都具有变量和函数等组件，这意味着您可以将从一种语言中学到的知识运用到另一种语言中。

在这篇博文中，您将学习一些今天就可以开始熟悉 Go 的方法。

基本功能

从高层次来看，函数是代码的构建块。 它们包含您的代码将要做的“事情”。 您的代码会返回hello world吗？ 是否要向 AWS 进行身份验证？ 它会制造机器人来统治世界吗？

内部包含函数的 Go 代码文件包含：

  1. 如果您看到main作为包名称，则意味着您正在使用的 Go 文件是应用程序堆栈的入口点。 如果您正在编写脚本或一些自动化代码，那么您的代码逻辑很可能位于main包内部。
  2. 例如，以下代码中使用的fmt包允许您执行各种功能，包括将某些内容（如字符串）打印到终端/控制台的能力。
  3. 在下面的示例中，它调用main函数，该函数通常用作子函数/子函数的入口点，您将在本博客文章的高级函数部分中了解这些函数。

要记住的一条重要信息是，当您定义main包时，它内部必须有一个main函数。 内部没有main包不会直接运行，它们是从main包运行的。 如果您尝试运行没有main函数的main包，您将收到类似于以下内容的错误。

 function main is undeclared in the main package

以下是基本 Go 函数的示例。

 package main import "fmt" func main() { fmt.Println("Hello World") }

高级功能

在上一节中，您了解了如何通过将大部分代码逻辑放入main函数内部来创建函数。 尽管您可能会在各种脚本和自动化功能中看到这一点，但您很可能不会看到主函数用作保存代码核心逻辑的函数。 相反，您很可能会看到子函数内部的核心代码逻辑。

子函数是在main函数之外创建的函数，通常由main函数调用。 为什么？ 简而言之，Go 是一种基于函数的编程语言。 它不使用方法或类，而是使用函数来实现其所有功能。 您可以使用main函数来调用子函数。

看一下下面的示例，您可以看到testing()函数是从main函数调用的。 然后main函数运行testing()函数。

 package main import "fmt" func main() { testing() } func testing() { fmt.Println("hello from the testing function") }

变量

学习函数之后，下一个逻辑步骤是学习变量。 变量是您可以在内存中保存随时间变化的数据的位置，而不是在代码中的某个位置硬编码该值。

例如，假设您有一个云区域，该区域可能会根据您的代码运行位置在某个时刻发生变化。 如果您将us-east-1之类的内容硬编码到代码中，这意味着每次在us-east-1之外运行代码时，您都必须仔细检查代码并更改它。 这是一个大麻烦。

相反，您可以创建一个包含值us-east-1变量，并且在整个代码中使用该变量时仅更改该值一次。

例如：

该变量存储在内存中，这意味着它是短暂的，您可以随时根据它的运行方式和位置来更改该值。

在本节中，您将了解在 go 中创建变量的三种主要方法。

速记

当您看到在 Go 中创建的变量时，您通常会看到它如下所示：

冒号后跟等号 ( := ) 表示正在定义变量。

下面是 Go 函数内部简写变量的示例。

 package main import "fmt" func main() { sayhi := "hello world" fmt.Println(sayhi) }

满的

尽管十有八九你会看到使用简写变量，但也有普通/完整变量。

完整变量包含：

  • 变量的名称
  • 变量类型（字符串、整型、浮点型等）
  • 价值

许多工程师喜欢完整变量，因为它告诉您并准确定义变量类型应该是什么，这可以在稍后未按预期返回正确值时进行故障排除节省大量时间。 您将在本博文的返回类型部分了解更多相关信息。

 package main import "fmt" func main() { var sayhi string = "hello world" fmt.Println(sayhi) }

全球的

创建变量的最后一种方式是全局变量。 请注意下面的代码示例， sayhi变量位于函数作用域之外。 原因是您有时可能想在多个函数中使用同一个变量。 如果变量存在于函数内部，则其值仅与该函数相关。 但是，如果变量位于函数外部，则它是全局变量，可以在整个代码中使用。

 package main import "fmt" var sayhi string = "hello world" func main() { testing() testing2() } func testing() { fmt.Println(sayhi) } func testing2() { fmt.Println(sayhi + " again") }

常数

常量本质上是硬编码的变量。 这是一个永远无法改变的价值。 使用变量，您可以在代码的一部分中定义值，然后在代码的另一部分中更改同一变量的值。 如果是常数，则该值永远不会改变。

如果您熟悉的话，它类似于 Python 中的tuple 。

下面的代码示例显示了 main 函数内部常量的示例。

该常量使用const关键字进行初始化。 请注意它看起来与普通变量几乎相同。 唯一的不同是您指定const关键字而不是var关键字。

 package main import "fmt" func main() { const hiagain string = "hello world" fmt.Println(hiagain) }

返回类型

在本博文的最后一部分，您将了解返回特定值的含义。 当您返回一个值时，这意味着您在代码内部指定函数应返回的内容。 它返回一个字符串吗？ 一个整数？ 代码的一些输出稍后会使用？

指定返回时，您将使用return关键字，后跟您想要从函数返回的内容。 请注意，当您返回如下代码所示的值时，您并不是专门将输出打印到屏幕上。 您只需获取该返回值以供稍后使用。

例如，也许您正在使用 Go 函数来初始化与 Kubernetes 集群的连接。 然后，当您在代码中运行特定任务时，可以使用该函数连接到 Kubernetes 集群。 如果您想使用 Go 创建 Kubernetes Pod，则必须连接到集群，您可以通过创建一个连接到集群并返回 Kubeconfig 的函数来实现这一点。

由于 Go 是静态类型语言，因此返回类型必须与返回的值匹配。 例如，如果您指定string作为返回类型并尝试返回int ，您将收到指示“类型不匹配”的错误。

 package main import "fmt" func main() { name("Mike") } func name(name string) string { fmt.Println(name) return name }

结论

这种语言确实具有“一刀切”的感觉，并且几乎可以用于任何事情。 如果您正在使用 Kubernetes，那么这是一门很好的语言，因为 Kubernetes 是用 Go 编写的。



原文:

As with all programming languages, there’s a plethora of options available to write the software or automation that you’re hoping to accomplish, which can get cumbersome for new programmers or someone that’s new to a language. Luckily, there’s always a place to start in terms of getting yourself up to speed with a new language. For example, all languages have components like variables and functions, which means you can take what you learn from one language and utilize it in another.

In this blog post, you’ll learn a few methods you can start today to get yourself familiar with Go.

Basic Functions

Functions, at a high level, are the building blocks of your code. They are what contain the “thing” that your code is going to do. Is your code going to return hello world? Is it going to authenticate to AWS? Is it going to build robots to take over the world?

A Go code file with a function inside of it contains:

  1. The package keyword followed by the package name. If you see main as the package name, that means the Go file you’re working with is the entry point into the application stack. The main package is what’s used to run all of the sub packages. If you’re writing a script or some automation code, chances are your code logic will be inside of the main package.
  2. The import keyword is how you can use standard and third-party libraries in your code. For example, the fmt package used in the below code allows you to perform various pieces of functionality, including the ability to print something (like a string) to the terminal/console.
  3. The func keyword defines a new function followed by the function name. In the example below, it’s calling the main function, which is typically used as the entry point into child/sub functions which you’ll learn about in the Advanced Functions section of this blog post.

One important piece of information to keep in mind is when you define a main package, it must have a main function inside of it. The main package is what’s run by something like the go run command. Packages that don’t have main inside of them aren’t run directly, they’re run from the main package. if you try to run a main package without a main function, you’ll get an error similar to the one below.

function main is undeclared in the main package

Below is an example of a basic Go function.

package main import "fmt" func main() { fmt.Println("Hello World")
}

Advanced Functions

In the previous section, you saw how functions are created by putting most of the code logic inside of the main function. Although you may see this for various scripts and automation capabilities, chances are you won’t see the main function used as the function that holds the core logic of the code. Instead, you’ll most likely see the core code logic inside of a child function.

Child functions are functions that are created outside of the main function and are usually called upon by the main function. Why? The short answer is that Go is a functional-based programing language. It doesn’t utilize methods or classes, it utilizes functions for all of its capabilities. The main function, much like the main package, is the entry point into the code. You can use the main function to call upon the child function(s).

Taking a look at the example below, you can see that the testing() function is called upon from the main function. The main function then runs the testing() function.

package main import "fmt" func main() { testing()
} func testing() { fmt.Println("hello from the testing function")
}

Variables

After learning functions, the next logical step is to learn variables. Variables are a location in which you can save data that will change over time in memory instead of hard coding the value somewhere in your code.

For example, let’s say you have a cloud region that may change at some point based on where your code is running. If you hard code something like us-east-1 into your code, that means every time you run the code outside of us-east-1, you have to go through the code and change it. It’s a major hassle.

Instead, you can create a variable that contains the value us-east-1 and only change the value once while using the variable throughout your code.

For example:

The variable is stored in memory, which means it’s ephemeral and you can always change the value based on how and where it’s running.

In this section, you’ll learn about three primary ways to create variables in go.

Shorthand

When you see a variable created in Go, you’ll typically see it look like the following:

The sayhi part is the key and the "hello world" part is the value (hence key/value pairs). The colon followed by the equals sign (:=) means a variable is being defined.

An example of the shorthand variable inside of a Go function is below.

package main import "fmt" func main() { sayhi := "hello world" fmt.Println(sayhi)
}

Full

Although 9 times out of 10 you’ll see the shorthand variable used, there’s also the longhand/full variable.

The full variable contains:

  • The var keyword
  • The name of the variable
  • The variable type (string, int, float, etc.)
  • The value

A lot of engineers like the full variable because it tells you and defines exactly what the variable type should be, which saves a lot of time in troubleshooting later on when the proper value isn’t returned as expected. Go is a statically typed language, which means whatever type you specify is the type that must be returned. You’ll learn more about that in the Return Types section of this blog post.

package main import "fmt" func main() { var sayhi string = "hello world" fmt.Println(sayhi)
}

Global

The last way in which you can create a variable is the global variable. Notice how with the code example below, the sayhi variable is outside of the function scope. The reason why is that you may have a time when you want to use the same variable inside of multiple functions. If the variable exists inside of a function, its value is tied to that function only. However, if the variable is outside of the function, it’s a global variable and can be used throughout your code.

package main import "fmt" var sayhi string = "hello world" func main() { testing() testing2()
} func testing() { fmt.Println(sayhi)
} func testing2() { fmt.Println(sayhi + " again")
}

Constants

A constant is essentially a variable that’s hard-coded. It’s a value that can never change. With a variable, you can define the value in one part of the code and then change the value of the same variable in another part of the code. With a constant, the value can never change.

It’s similar to a tuple in Python if you’re familiar.

The code example below shows an example of a constant inside of a main function.

The constant is initialized by using the const keyword. Notice how it looks almost identical to the longhand variable. The only different is you’re specifying the const keyword instead of the var keyword.

package main import "fmt" func main() { const hiagain string = "hello world" fmt.Println(hiagain)
}

Return Types

For the last section of this blog post, you’ll learn about what it means to return a specific value. When you return a value, it means that you’re specifying inside of your code what the function should be returning. Is it returning a string? An int? Some output of the code to be used later on?

When specifying a return, you’ll use the return keyword followed by what you want to be returned from the function. Please note that when you return a value like in the code below, you’re not specifically printing output to the screen. You’re simply taking that returned value to be used later on.

For example, perhaps you’re using a Go function to initialize a connection to a Kubernetes cluster. You then use said function to connect to the Kubernetes cluster when you’re running a specific task in your code. If you want to use Go to create a Kubernetes Pod, you would have to connect to the cluster, and you can do that by creating a function that connects to the cluster and returns the Kubeconfig.

Because Go is a statically typed language, the return type must match the value that’s being returned. For example, if you specify string as the return type and try to return an int, you’ll get an error that indicates a “type mismatch”.

package main import "fmt" func main() { name("Mike")
} func name(name string) string { fmt.Println(name) return name
}

Conclusion

Go is an amazing programming language for anything and everything system-related, application-related, and automation-related. It’s a language that certainly has a “one size fits all” feeling and can be used for just about anything. If you’re working with Kubernetes, it’s a great language to know as Kubernetes is written in Go.
item.author: thenjdevopsguy

item.title: 数据属性：包含额外的 HTML 元素信息 | Data Attributes: como armazenar informação extra em elementos HTML
item.description:
因迪斯

什么是数据属性？

数据属性是HTML元素中不允许的附加信息。 星号是用 um nome descritivo para o bad que você deseja armazenar 替换的。 data-nome-descritivo="dado"

例如：产品列表和列表中的产品列表。

 <ul id="lista-de-produtos"> <li data-produto-id="1">Produto A</li> <li data-produto-id="2">Produto B</li> <li data-produto-id="3">Produto C</li> </ul>

没有示例，使用 o 数据属性data-produto-id para armazenar o ID de cada produto。 重要的是数据属性与元素无关或相互关联，这些属性是个性化信息的重要组成部分。

没有HTML ，分开 palavras com um traço '-'
data-produto-id

没有JavaScript ，访问 esse data-* utilizando letra maiúscula
dataset.produtoId

用于使用数据属性吗？

使用 JavaScript 和 CSS 进行后处理。

  • 应用不同的主题或 um elemento com 基础没有勇气执行data-* 。

    • 围绕数据属性的语法进行讨论，您可以探索这些数据并在我们的项目中使用。

    在 JavaScript 中操作 DOM：

    可以使用 JavaScript 来选择、链接和操作特定的 DOM 元素的数据属性。

    例如：如果您在推特上转发推文，则转发推文的目标是一条推文。

     <div id="tweet-1"> <p>Hello twitter world!</p> </div> <button id="retweet-btn">Retweet</button>

    您可以使用data-*来发送推文或转发推文。

     <div id="tweet-1"> <p>Hello twitter world!</p> </div> <button id="retweet-btn" data-retweet="tweet-1">Retweet</button>

    无需 JavaScript 访问

    访问无 CSS

    您可以使用 CSS 来选择数据属性，以更改数据属性的元素。

    以推文为例，CSS 可以代表转发推文和转发推文。

     [data-retweet="tweet-1"] { color: red; }

    结论

    总结一下，数据属性是一个新的功能实现项目。 探索和使用数据属性，您可以根据项目的需要进行使用和调整。

    Espero ter ajudado a entender melhor um pouquinhos sobre Data Attributes eo poder que eles possuem para turbinar o seu projeto novo! Espero ter ajudado a entender melhor um pouquinhos sobre Data Attributes eo poder que eles possuem para turbinar o seu projeto novo!



    原文:

    Quando eu estava desenvolvendo um projeto chamado 'Twimba', um clone do feed do twitter eu me deparei com o seguinte problema: 'Como vincular o tweet ao botão de like e retweet?' Vem comigo que te explico como consegui usando Data Attributes!

    Índice

    O que são Data Attributes?

    Data Attributes é um recurso que nos permite adicionar informação adicional aos elementos HTML. Sua syntax segue esse padrão data-*. O asterisco pode ser substituído por um nome descritivo para o dado que você deseja armazenar. data-nome-descritivo="dado"

    Por exemplo: Temos uma lista de produtos e queremos armazenar um id único para cada um dos produtos na lista.

    <ul id="lista-de-produtos"> <li data-produto-id="1">Produto A</li> <li data-produto-id="2">Produto B</li> <li data-produto-id="3">Produto C</li>
</ul>

    No exemplo acima, utilizamos o Data Attribute data-produto-id para armazenar o ID de cada produto. O valor desse atributo pode ser qualquer dado que você queira associar ao elemento. É importante ressaltar que os Data Attributes não afetam a aparência ou o comportamento padrão dos elementos, eles são apenas um meio de armazenar informações personalizadas.

    Melhor prática para nomear data-*

    No HTML, separe palavras com um traço '-'
    data-produto-id

    No JavaScript, acesse esse data-* utilizando letra maiúscula
    dataset.produtoId

    E no CSS, coloque o data-* entre colchetes
    [data-produto-id="1"]

    Por quê usar Data Attributes?

    data-* attributes é uma maneira eficaz de armazenar dados que não são visíveis para os usuários, mas podem ser utilizados por nós desenvolvedores para realizar ações específicas ou aplicar estilos com base no valor do atributo.

    Alguns casos de uso comuns para os atributos data-* incluem:

    • Armazenar dados para serem acessados posteriormente pelo JavaScript e/ou CSS.
    • Fornecer metadados sobre um elemento que são necessários para determinadas funcionalidades (por exemplo, armazenar a data de início de um evento no calendário).
    • Aplicar diferentes temas ou estilos a um elemento com base no valor do atributo data-*.

    Usando Data Attributes em Projetos:

    Agora que entendemos o conceito e a syntax dos Data Attributes, vamos explorar como eles são úteis e como utilizá-los em nossos projetos.

    Manipulação do DOM com JavaScript:

    Podemos usar Data Attributes para selecionar, vincular e manipular elementos específicos no DOM usando JavaScript.

    Por exemplo: Aqui temos um tweet com um botão de retweetar, nosso objetivo é fazer com que o botão de retweetar esteja vinculado a esse tweet.

    <div id="tweet-1"> <p>Hello twitter world!</p>
</div>
<button id="retweet-btn">Retweet</button>

    Aqui podemos utilizar um data-* para vincular o id do tweet ao botão de retweet.

    <div id="tweet-1"> <p>Hello twitter world!</p>
</div>
<button id="retweet-btn" data-retweet="tweet-1">Retweet</button>

    Acesso no JavaScript

    Podemos acessar o valor desse data-* com a propriedade dataset do JavaScript:

    const retweetBtn = document.getElementById('retweet-btn'); retweetBtn.dataset.retweet // output: "tweet-1"

    Dando um console.log() no retweetBtn.dataset.share conseguimos acessar o id do tweet-1.

    Acesso no CSS

    Podemos usar os seletores de Data Attributes em CSS para alterar o estilo do elemento de acordo com o Data Attribute.

    Ainda usando o exemplo do tweet, podemos criar um estilo de CSS para representar quando o tweet foi retweetado e mudar a cor do botão.

    [data-retweet="tweet-1"] { color: red;
}

    Conclusão

    Em resumo, Data Attributes é uma ótima ferramenta para implementar um feature novo no seu projeto. Ao explorar e dominar o uso de Data Attributes, você poderá criar experiências de usuário mais envolventes e adaptáveis às necessidades do seu projeto.

    Espero ter ajudado a entender melhor um pouquinhos sobre Data Attributes e o poder que eles possuem para turbinar o seu projeto novo!

item.title: Nestjs 课程 - 第 02 课 - MVC 模式简介 | Nestjs Course - Lesson 02 - Introduction to the MVC pattern
item.description:
发表于 7 月 2 日

  1. 初步设计需求分析
  2. 设置项目结构
  3. 功能细节

    数据库设计

    设置项目结构

    1. 使用 NestJS 创建一个新项目
    2. 根据模块结构和MVC模型的项目文件夹结构
    3. 使用 VSCode 调试 NestJS 项目

    使用 NestJS 创建一个新项目

    nest new nestjs-tutorial-2023 --skip-install cd nestjs-tutorial-2023 npm i

    初始化项目后，项目的目录树结构如下图所示：

    项目目录结构按照模块结构和MVC模型

    1. 安装NestJS以支持模板引擎EJS

    我们回顾一下expressjs的架构，需要设置一些参数，如下：

    • 模板引擎：一些流行的模板引擎，如 pug、ejs、hbs
    • 而在 NestJS 中，我们将如何做到呢？

       npm install --save @nestjs/serve-static npm i --save ejs
       // src/app.module.ts import { Module } from "@nestjs/common"; import { AppController } from "./app.controller"; import { AppService } from "./app.service"; import { ServeStaticModule } from "@nestjs/serve-static"; import { join } from "path"; @Module({ imports: [ // public folder ServeStaticModule.forRoot({ rootPath: join(process.cwd(), "public"), // Your asset will be placed at : http://localhost:1337/public/assets/main.css serveRoot: "/public", }), ], controllers: [AppController], providers: [AppService], }) export class AppModule {}
       // src/main.ts import { NestFactory } from "@nestjs/core"; import { AppModule } from "./app.module"; import { NestExpressApplication } from "@nestjs/platform-express"; import { join } from "path"; async function bootstrap() { const app = await NestFactory.create<NestExpressApplication>(AppModule); // app.useStaticAssets(join(process.cwd(), 'public')); app.setViewEngine("ejs"); app.setBaseViewsDir([join(process.cwd(), "views")]); await app.listen(3000); } bootstrap();
       // src/app.controller.ts import { Controller, Get, Render } from "@nestjs/common"; import { AppService } from "./app.service"; @Controller() export class AppController { constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {} @Get() @Render("index") homePage() { return {}; } }

      视图/index.ejs

       <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8" /> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /> <title><%= title %></title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="/public/assets/main.css" /> </head> <body> <h1><%= title %></h1> <img src="/public/assets/nestjs-tutorial-2023.png" alt="NestJS Tutorial 2023" /> <script src="/public/assets/main.js"></script> </body> </html>

      这就是你得到的

      参考

一旦被暂停，nextjsvietnam 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，nextjsvietnam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，nextjsvietnam 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 nextjsvietnam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 nextjsvietnam：

使 nextjsvietnam 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 nextjsvietnam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

NextjsVietnam

Posted on Jul 2

  1. Preliminary design requirements analysis
  2. Setting up the project structure
  3. Overview of classes in the MVC model and code practice

Functional details

Database Design

Set up project structure

  1. Create a new project with NestJS
  2. Project folder structure according to module structure and MVC model
  3. Debug NestJS project with VSCode

Create a new project with NestJS 

nest new nestjs-tutorial-2023 --skip-install
cd nestjs-tutorial-2023
npm i

After initializing the project, the project's directory tree structure will look like the image below:

Project directory structure according to the module structure and MVC model

  1. Install NestJS to support template Engine EJS

Let's go back a bit with the architecture of expressjs, you need to set some parameters as follows:

  • Template Engine: some popular template engines like pug, ejs, hbs
  • Static Assets: folder containing static files such as css, js, images of the website 
const express = require("express");
const app = express();
const port = process.env.PORT || 1337;
const path = require("path"); // static assets
const publicAssetsPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), "public");
// using express static middleware
app.use("/public", express.static(publicAssetsPath));
// Your asset will be placed at : http://localhost:1337/public/assets/main.css // select view engine is ejs
app.set("view engine", "ejs"); // set view folder
const viewsPath = process.cwd() + "/views";
app.set("views", [viewsPath]); // global variable through app
app.locals = { title: "MVC Tutorial",
}; // home page
app.get("/", (req, res) => { res.render("index", { heading: "Home page" });
}); app.listen(port, () => { console.log(`Example app listening at http://localhost:${port}`);
});

And in NestJS, how will we do it?

npm install --save @nestjs/serve-static
npm i --save ejs

// src/app.module.ts
import { Module } from "@nestjs/common";
import { AppController } from "./app.controller";
import { AppService } from "./app.service";
import { ServeStaticModule } from "@nestjs/serve-static";
import { join } from "path"; @Module({ imports: [ // public folder ServeStaticModule.forRoot({ rootPath: join(process.cwd(), "public"), // Your asset will be placed at : http://localhost:1337/public/assets/main.css serveRoot: "/public", }), ], controllers: [AppController], providers: [AppService],
})
export class AppModule {}

// src/main.ts
import { NestFactory } from "@nestjs/core";
import { AppModule } from "./app.module";
import { NestExpressApplication } from "@nestjs/platform-express";
import { join } from "path"; async function bootstrap() { const app = await NestFactory.create<NestExpressApplication>(AppModule); // app.useStaticAssets(join(process.cwd(), 'public')); app.setViewEngine("ejs"); app.setBaseViewsDir([join(process.cwd(), "views")]); await app.listen(3000);
}
bootstrap();

// src/app.controller.ts import { Controller, Get, Render } from "@nestjs/common";
import { AppService } from "./app.service"; @Controller()
export class AppController { constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {} @Get() @Render("index") homePage() { return {}; }
}

views/index.ejs

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8" /> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /> <title><%= title %></title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="/public/assets/main.css" /> </head> <body> <h1><%= title %></h1> <img src="/public/assets/nestjs-tutorial-2023.png" alt="NestJS Tutorial 2023" /> <script src="/public/assets/main.js"></script> </body>
</html>

And here is what you get

References

Once suspended, nextjsvietnam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, nextjsvietnam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by nextjsvietnam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If nextjsvietnam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag nextjsvietnam:

Make all posts by nextjsvietnam less visible

nextjsvietnam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging nextjsvietnam will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: Git 开源的未来 | Git the Future of Open Source
item.description:
近年来，开源软件取得了巨大的发展势头，彻底改变了软件的开发和共享方式。

这一运动的核心是 Git，它是一种分布式版本控制系统 (DVCS)，已成为全球开源项目不可或缺的一部分。

本文探讨了 Git 在塑造开源软件未来方面的重要性，强调了它对提高安全性、降低成本、创新、协作和透明度的好处和贡献。

开源软件的好处

开源软件具有多种优势，使其越来越受欢迎。 首先，它增强了安全性，因为源代码可访问并可由全球社区审核，从而降低了漏洞被忽视的风险。

此外，开源软件通过消除许可费用并允许组织定制和修改代码来满足其需求，从而显着降低了成本。

此外，这些项目的开放性使开发人员能够在现有解决方案的基础上进行构建，从而推动快速进步和突破，从而促进创新。

开源软件还促进协作，因为来自不同背景和地点的开发人员可以为项目贡献他们的专业知识，从而培育一个多元化且充满活力的社区。

最后，开源软件的透明度使用户能够了解软件的工作原理，从而促进信任和问责制。

它允许多个开发人员同时在同一个代码库上工作，无论他们的地理位置如何。

使用 Git，开发人员可以无缝管理和合并更改，减少冲突并确保简化的开发流程。

其分散式架构使开发人员能够离线工作，提供灵活性并实现更快的开发周期。

它提供了与 Git 类似的去中心化工作流程，并支持广泛的平台。 它也称为SVN，是一种广泛应用于企业环境的集中式版本控制系统。 与Git不同，SVN遵循客户端-服务器架构，适合需要严格访问控制和集中管理的项目。 对于初学者和需要简单 VCS 的团队来说，这是一个不错的选择。

  • 它为那些喜欢可视化方法的人提供了用户友好的命令行界面和图形用户界面。 要下载集市，请访问：
  • 这些功能包括基于角色的访问控制、分支和合并工具以及内置问题跟踪器。
  • 它提供了许多独立 Git 客户端所不具备的功能，例如问题跟踪、wiki 和持续集成。
  • 它提供简单的命令行界面、内置 Web 界面和紧凑的存储格式。 它提供与 GitLab 类似的功能，但有一个针对开源项目的免费计划。

最终，Git 的最佳替代方案将取决于您的具体需求和偏好。

如果您正在寻找类似于 Git 的免费开源 VCS，那么 Mercurial 或 Bazaar 可能是不错的选择。

如果您需要具有更多功能或基于 Web 的界面的 VCS，GitLab 或 Bitbucket 可能会更好。

开源软件的未来

开源软件的未来看起来充满希望，各行业的采用率持续上升。

由于其强大的功能和广泛的采用，Git 有望成为该领域领先的 DVCS。

此外，Git 被创新地用于持续集成和持续交付 (CI/CD)，简化软件开发生命周期并实现快速部署。

随着对云计算、移动设备、安全、隐私和高效开发流程的需求不断增加，Git 的可扩展性、可靠性和易用性使其成为满足这些不断变化的需求的关键工具。

结论

凭借其分布式版本控制功能，Git 使开发人员能够有效协作、管理大型复杂项目并保护代码完整性。

在提高安全性、降低成本、创新、协作和透明度等优势的推动下，开源软件正在蓬勃发展，而 Git 是这一成功的关键推动者。

随着开源运动继续塑造软件行业，Git 的可访问性、可扩展性和多功能性使其成为推动未来进步的重要工具。

在 Git 的掌舵下，开源软件的未来将变得更加易于访问、安全和创新。

如果您觉得这篇文章令人兴奋，请在Learnhub 博客上找到更多类似的令人兴奋的文章； 我们编写从云计算到前端开发、网络安全、人工智能和区块链的所有技术。

资源



原文:

Open-source software has gained significant momentum in recent years, revolutionizing how software is developed and shared.

At the heart of this movement lies Git, a distributed version control system (DVCS) that has become integral to open-source projects worldwide.

This article explores the significance of Git in shaping the future of open-source software, highlighting its benefits and contributions to increased security, reduced costs, innovation, collaboration, and transparency.

The Benefits of Open-Source Software

Open-source software offers several advantages that contribute to its growing popularity. Firstly, it enhances security as the source code is accessible and can be audited by a global community, reducing the risk of vulnerabilities going unnoticed.

Additionally, open-source software significantly reduces costs by eliminating licensing fees and allowing organizations to customize and modify the code to meet their needs.

Moreover, the open nature of these projects fosters innovation by enabling developers to build upon existing solutions, driving rapid advancements and breakthroughs.

Open source software also promotes collaboration, as developers from different backgrounds and locations can contribute their expertise to a project, fostering a diverse and vibrant community.

Finally, the transparency of open-source software allows users to understand how the software works, promoting trust and accountability.

The Role of Git in Open-Source Software

Git, as a DVCS, plays a crucial role in enabling collaboration and facilitating open-source software development.

It allows multiple developers to work concurrently on the same codebase, regardless of their geographical location.

With Git, developers can seamlessly manage and merge changes, reducing conflicts and ensuring a streamlined development process.

Its decentralized architecture empowers developers to work offline, providing flexibility and enabling faster development cycles.

Git has become the preferred version control system for numerous renowned open-source projects, including the Linux kernel, Firefox, and Android, attesting to its reliability and scalability.

Alternative to Git

  • Mercurial is a distributed version control system known for its simplicity and ease of use. It offers a similar decentralized workflow to Git and supports a wide range of platforms. Mercurial provides robust branching and merging capabilities, making it suitable for projects of all sizes.
  • Subversion (SVN) is a centralized version control system older than Git. It is also known as SVN, a centralized version control system widely used in enterprise settings. Unlike Git, SVN follows a client-server architecture, making it suitable for projects requiring strict access control and centralized management. SVN offers comprehensive revision history and robust branching and merging capabilities.
  • Bazaar is a decentralized version control system known for its ease of use and flexibility. It is a good choice for beginners and teams that need a simple VCS.
  • It offers a user-friendly command-line interface and a graphical user interface for those who prefer a visual approach. Bazaar supports various workflows and provides seamless integration with other tools. To download Bazaar, visit:
  • Plastic SCM is a commercial version control system that offers a number of features that are not available in free Git alternatives. These features include role-based access control, branching and merging tools, and a built-in issue tracker.
  • GitLab is a web-based Git repository hosting service. It offers a number of features that are not available in the standalone Git client, such as issue tracking, wikis, and continuous integration.
  • Fossil is a distributed version control system that combines version control, bug tracking, and wiki functionality in a single integrated package. It offers a simple command-line interface, a built-in web interface, and a compact storage format. Fossil is lightweight and easy to set up, making it an attractive choice for small to medium-sized projects.
  • Bitbucket is another web-based Git repository hosting service. It offers similar features to GitLab but has a free plan for open-source projects.

Ultimately, the best alternative to Git for you will depend on your specific needs and preferences.

If you are looking for a free and open-source VCS similar to Git, then Mercurial or Bazaar may be a good choice.

GitLab or Bitbucket may be better if you need a VCS with more features or a web-based interface.

The Future of Open-Source Software

The future of open-source software looks promising, with its adoption continuing to rise across industries.

Git is well-positioned to be the leading DVCS in this landscape due to its robust features and widespread adoption.

Furthermore, Git is being leveraged innovatively for continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD), streamlining the software development lifecycle and enabling rapid deployment.

Git's accessibility and security measures make open-source software more inclusive and protected, attracting more contributors and users.

As the demand for cloud computing, mobile devices, security, privacy, and efficient development processes increases, Git's scalability, reliability, and ease of use position it as a pivotal tool to meet these evolving needs.

Conclusion

Git's influence on the future of open-source software cannot be overstated. With its distributed version control capabilities, Git empowers developers to collaborate effectively, manage large and complex projects, and safeguard code integrity.

Open source software, driven by the advantages of increased security, reduced costs, innovation, collaboration, and transparency, is thriving, and Git is a key enabler in this success.

As the open source movement continues to shape the software industry, Git's accessibility, scalability, and versatility make it a vital tool in driving future advancements.

With Git at the helm, the future of open-source software is set to be even more accessible, secure, and innovative.

If you find this post exciting, find more exciting posts like this on Learnhub Blog; we write everything tech from Cloud computing to Frontend Dev, Cybersecurity, AI and Blockchain.

Resource
item.author: scofieldidehen

item.title: 在 4 个 Node.js 框架中实现授权 | Implementing Authorization in 4 Node.js Frameworks
item.description:
作为 Web 开发人员，确保应用程序的安全是我们的首要任务之一。 安全的主要支柱之一是实施强大的授权机制，以有效管理用户权限。 在这篇博文中，我们将总结并提供一些最佳资源的链接，以指导您向各种 Node.js/框架添加授权。

让我们深入研究每一篇文章：

本指南探讨了将 RBAC（基于角色的访问控制）集成到 Express 应用程序中的过程。 这种结构有助于管理具有多个用户和角色的复杂系统，为权限控制提供高粒度。

本文将指导您创建基本的 Express.js 应用程序、设置具有角色的用户模型、定义角色权限以及创建中间件以在访问某些路由之前检查用户权限。 通过遵循本指南，您可以成功地将 RBAC 集成到 Express.js 应用程序中，从而使您的应用程序安全且易于管理。

与 Express.js 一样，将 RBAC 集成到 Next.js 应用程序中可以显着增强其安全性。 本指南提供了有关为 Next.js 设置 RBAC 的详细说明。

本文遵循与 Express.js 指南类似的流程，但由于两个框架之间的差异而存在一些变化。 它包括有关创建 Next.js 应用程序、设置用户模型、定义角色和权限以及创建授权中间件的指南。 按照本指南使用强大的授权机制来强化您的 Next.js 应用程序。

为了补充这种效率，有效的授权机制至关重要。 本文探讨如何为 Fastify 创建授权中间件。

本文深入介绍了 Fastify 独特的中间件方法以及如何利用它为您的应用程序构建强大的授权。 它解释了如何定义用户角色和权限，如何使用 Fastify 的钩子创建授权中间件，以及如何应用此中间件来保护应用程序的路由。 深入研究本指南，为您的 Fastify 应用程序添加有效的授权。

本指南演示如何使用 RBAC 保护 NestJS 应用程序内的 URL。

本文介绍了如何设置 NestJS 应用程序、定义角色和权限以及创建路由保护防护。 该防护充当授权中间件，根据用户的角色和权限验证用户是否可以访问某些 URL。 查看本指南以增强 NestJS 应用程序的安全性。

总之，实施强大的授权机制对于任何应用程序来说都是一项重要的安全措施。 根据您选择的 Node.js/JS 框架，这些资源提供了有关如何集成 RBAC 以安全高效地管理用户权限的分步说明。 快乐编码！



原文:

As web developers, ensuring the security of our applications is one of our top priorities. One of the main pillars of security is implementing a robust authorization mechanism that efficiently manages user permissions. In this blog post, we will summarize and provide links to some of the best resources to guide you in adding authorization to various Node.js/ frameworks.

Let's delve into each article:

1. Implement RBAC Authorization in Express

Express.js is a popular minimalist web application framework for Node.js. This guide explores the process of integrating RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) into an Express application. RBAC is a popular authorization scheme where permissions are associated with roles, and users are assigned roles to gain certain permissions. This structure helps manage complex systems with multiple users and roles, providing a high level of granularity for permission control.

The article guides you through creating a basic Express.js app, setting up user models with roles, defining role permissions, and creating middlewares to check user permissions before accessing certain routes. By following this guide, you can successfully integrate RBAC into your Express.js app, making your application secure and manageable.

2. How to Add RBAC in Next.js

Next.js, a React framework, is gaining significant popularity due to its simplicity and performance features. As with Express.js, integrating RBAC into a Next.js application significantly enhances its security. This guide provides detailed instructions on setting up RBAC for Next.js.

The article follows a similar flow as the Express.js guide but with some variations due to the differences between the two frameworks. It includes guidance on creating a Next.js app, setting up user models, defining roles and permissions, and creating an authorization middleware. Follow this guide to fortify your Next.js application with a robust authorization mechanism.

3. How to Create an Authorization Middleware for Fastify

Fastify, a high-performance web framework for Node.js, prides itself on its speed and low overhead. To complement this efficiency, an effective authorization mechanism is crucial. This article explores how to create an authorization middleware for Fastify.

The article provides insights into Fastify's unique approach to middleware and how it can be harnessed to build robust authorization for your application. It explains how to define user roles and permissions, how to create authorization middleware using Fastify's hooks, and how to apply this middleware to secure your application's routes. Dive into this guide to add efficient authorization to your Fastify application.

4. How to Protect a URL Inside a NestJS App Using RBAC Authorization

NestJS, a progressive Node.js framework for building efficient and scalable server-side applications, is highly extensible and supports modularization, making it suitable for complex applications. This guide demonstrates how to protect a URL inside a NestJS application using RBAC.

The article goes over how to set up your NestJS application, define roles and permissions, and create guards for route protection. The guard acts as an authorization middleware, validating if a user can access certain URLs based on their role and permissions. Check out this guide to enhance your NestJS application's security.

In conclusion, implementing a robust authorization mechanism is an essential security measure for any application. Depending on your choice of Node.js/JS framework, these resources provide step-by-step instructions on how to integrate RBAC for secure and efficient management of user permissions. Happy coding!
